title: Understanding Constant Velocity
text: When an object moves at a constant velocity, what can we say about the net force acting on it?

title: Net Force at Constant Velocity
text: If an object is moving at a constant velocity, the net force acting on it is zero. This means the upward force (tension) must balance the downward force (gravity).

title: Calculating Gravitational Force
text: The gravitational force acting on the elevator can be calculated using the formula: $$F_{gravity}=m g$$, where $$m$$ is the mass and g is the acceleration due to gravity $$\frac{9.8m}{s^2}$$.

title: Calculate Gravitational Force
text: Calculate the gravitational force acting on the elevator using the formula $$F_{gravity}=m g$$.

title: Tension Equals Gravitational Force
text: Since the elevator is moving at a constant velocity, the tension in the cable must equal the gravitational force acting on the elevator.
